title: PagerDuty incident summary
description: One record per incident over a bounded look-back window: the incident's opaque id and number, urgency, status, the affected service identity, and the created / resolved timestamps. Pulled read-only from the PagerDuty REST API (incidents endpoint, since/until window). SUMMARY metadata ONLY — never the incident's free-text title, body, notes, customer data, postmortem text, or any responder personal contact detail. Proves incidents are detected, handled, and resolved (SOC 2 CC7.3/CC7.4/CC7.5; the incident-handling concept SCF labels IRO-02, here mapped to the present catalog anchor IRO-09 Incident Reporting). The connector reports a descriptive incident posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9); the spec candidate IRO-02 is absent from the bundled SCF catalog fixture, so IRO-09 — the closest present incident anchor — is used (see slice 654 decisions-log D2).

incident_id.description: PagerDuty-native stable identifier of the incident (an opaque ID, not a credential).

service_id.description: Opaque id of the affected PagerDuty service (non-secret label).

service_name.description: Display name of the affected service. A service name, never incident free-text.

resolved_at.description: When the incident was resolved (RFC 3339); absent if not yet resolved.
